Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a specific event log of a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process I need to identify cases with long resolution times find the root causes delays and provide recommendations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Mental or Scratchpad Calculation Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Duration 2 hours 15 mins Flow Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Resolve Close Standard flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Duration 25 hours 10 mins Flow Receive Triage Assign L1 Escalate to L2 Investigate Resolve Next day Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key observation Assign L1 09 00 to Escalate 11 30 2 5 hours gap Escalate 11 30 to Investigate 14 00 2 5 hours gap Investigate 14 00 Day 1 to Resolve 09 00 Day 2 Overnight long duration investigation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Duration 1 hour 20 mins Flow Fast standard

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Duration 24 hours 10 mins Flow Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Resolve Next day Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key observation No escalation mentioned in the log explicitly as an event like Escalate to but the times are long Wait 1 Receive to Triage 40m Wait 2 Triage to Assign 30m Wait 3 Assign to Investigate 3 5 hours 09 30 to 13 00 Investigate to Resolve Overnight 13 00 Day 1 to 08 00 Day 2

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Duration 49 hours Flow Receive Triage Assign L1 Investigate Escalate L2 Long Gap Investigate L2 Resolve Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key observation Escalation happens after initial investigation unlike 102 where it happened somewhat quickly after assign Gap between Escalate 10 00 Day 1 and Next Investigate 14 00 Day 2 That is a 28 hour wait just to start the Level 2 investigation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings for Task 1 Fast 101 2h 103 1 3h Slow 102 25h 104 24h 105 49h Conclusion Cases 102 104 and 105 are the outliers Case 105 is the most critical

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings for Task 2 Root Causes Factor A Escalations Case 102 and 105 involve Escalate to Level 2 Case 105 has a massive delay specifically after the escalation event It took over a day for the Level 2 agent to pick it up

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Factor B Waiting Times Bottlenecks Case 104 No explicit escalation event but maybe implicit complexity Huge delay between Assignment 09 30 and Investigation 13 00 Then Investigation spans overnight Case 102 Delay between Escalation 11 30 and Investigation 14 00 though this is reasonable 2 5 hrs the main driver is likely the overnight resolution time or the pre escalation wait Case 105 The Escalation Handoff is broken The ticket sat idle for 28 hours after being escalated

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Factor C Investigation Duration In slow cases 102 104 105 the Investigate Issue phase either starts late or takes a very long time spanning days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Why delays happen L2 Backlog Availability In Case 105 the escalation happened at 10 AM but L2 did not look at it until 2 PM the next day Suggests Level 2 team is overloaded or works different shifts hours without proper handover L1 Inefficiency Stalling In Case 104 and partly 102 there are long gaps before the agent even starts working

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Review Level 2 staffing capacity Implement SLAs for Time to Pick Up after escalation Check why Case 104 stalled for 3 5 hours before investigation Agent availability

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Deep Dive into specific bottlenecks Case 102 The escalation happened relatively early The delay is largely in the Investigate phase 2pm Day 1 to 9am Day 2 19 hours Also a 2 5h gap before escalation Case 104 No escalation step logged but behaves like a complex issue 3 5h wait to start investigating 19h investigation time Case 105 The killer is the 28 hour delay between Escalation Day 1 10 00 and Investigation Day 2 14 00 This is the smoking gun for Escalation Bottleneck
